coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy shakeout, 45–50 min at Z2 (140–150 bpm), no faster. You're 28 hours from your long run tomorrow and need to prime the legs without burning glycogen or adding fatigue. Keep it flat or rolling; this is movement, not work. Hit 6:00–6:30/km pace and dial it back if you feel sluggish—that's a signal your aerobic system is still recovering from Wednesday's vert block.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Front-load carbs today: 1.5–2g/kg bodyweight in the 24 hours leading into tomorrow's long run. Since you're traveling constantly, prioritize easily digestible sources (rice, pasta, oats, bananas) and hit 500–600g total. During today's easy run, take nothing—you're Z2 for under an hour. Tonight, eat a solid dinner 3 hours before bed with protein + carbs; hydrate aggressively but taper 90 min before sleep to avoid nocturnal bathroom trips that trash sleep quality.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Aim for 8–8.5 hours tonight; you're in peak phase and tomorrow is a big day. Avoid screens 30 min before bed, keep the room cool (18–19°C), and if travel is involved, use your own pillow if possible—sleep consistency beats perfect conditions. Friday night is non-negotiable for an ultra athlete running 6 days/week. If you're tempted to work late, remember: sleep is a performance substrate, not a luxury.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 15 weeks out from the Julian Alps 50K. Tomorrow's long run will test alpine fitness; visualize Kranjska Gora's technical descents and sparse aid stations while running. Use the shakeout today to settle your nervous system and confirm your legs feel fresh—if they don't, that's data for adjusting tomorrow's intensity or duration. Treat peak phase as your dress rehearsal, not a grind.
